page: Google Cloud Platform n2-highmem-48 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs derived from 24 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled, operating at a base clock speed of 2.8 GHz. The processor includes 33 MB of L3 cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 KB of L1d cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 (including AVX512F and AVX512_VNNI) and AES cryptography. The instance is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs.

Performance benchmarks place this instance in the strong tier for memory bandwidth and web throughput. Uncached large-block memory read bandwidth reaches 212.65 GB/sec, and write bandwidth reaches 98.19 GB/sec, both ranking in the top 25% of tested servers. Static web serving throughput is also strong at 47.78 GB/sec. Other compute metrics, such as stress-ng multi-core CPU performance (45,193.17) and Geekbench multi-core scores (11,383.0), fall within the average range. The system demonstrates reliable multi-threaded capabilities, as evidenced by a bzip3 multi-threaded decompression speed of 67.59 GB/sec and Redis SET performance of 45.48 million operations per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the n2-highmem-48 offers high resource density for memory-bound applications without the overhead of specialized GPU hardware. The 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it highly suitable for enterprise databases, large-scale caching layers, in-memory data processing, and software compilation. It is also well-suited for high-throughput static web serving and moderate multi-threaded workloads that require substantial memory capacity alongside dedicated Intel Xeon compute resources.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highmem-48 is an x86_64 virtual machine instance configured with 48 dedicated vCPUs and 384.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. Powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.8 GHz, it features 24 physical cores, a 33 MB L3 cache, and supports AVX-512 instructions. This instance does not include local storage or GPUs. Benchmark data highlights strong performance in uncached large-block memory bandwidth, with reads at 212.65 GB/sec and writes at 98.19 GB/sec, alongside a high static web serving throughput of 47.78 GB/sec. Other compute and database benchmarks fall within the average range. The n2-highmem-48 is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive workloads, making it highly suitable for large databases, in-memory caching, software compilation, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highmem-48 is an Intel Xeon-powered instance with 48 vCPUs and 384 GB RAM. It delivers strong memory bandwidth and is optimized for memory-intensive database and web serving workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highmem-48 features 48 vCPUs and 384 GB RAM, offering strong memory bandwidth for database and enterprise workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and high memory bandwidth for demanding database and enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated vCPUs and 384 GB of RAM providing 8 GB per core
Intel Xeon x86_64 processor running at 2.8 GHz with AVX-512 support
Strong uncached memory read bandwidth of 212.65 GB/sec
High static web serving throughput of 47.78 GB/sec
Optimized for large databases, in-memory caching, and software compilation
